records.pText:
These denizens of
the mountains and
forests pose
little threat
alone, but spell
trouble in groups.
Their Charge
attacks can be
somewhat hard to
deal with.

Rather intelligent
for their kind,
these goblins use
both swords and
shields. They are
resolute in
purpose and use
Guard to raise
their physical
defense in battle.

Very intelligent
for their kind,
these goblins are
skilled archers.
Their Sharpshoot
ability makes them
dangerous foes.

More wicked and
cunning than
goblins, these
small humanoids
silence foes with
<Withering
Silence>, steal BP
with Brave Robber,
and use other
dirty tricks.

These pretentious
kobolds think they
are knights just
because they can
use a sword and
shield. They are
known to use
Shield Bash to
paralyze their
foes.

These pretentious
kobolds think they
are rangers. They
use bows and can
even fire two
arrows at a time
with Double Shot,
but they are still
no match for a
seasoned ranger.

These sturdy, pig-
faced humanoids
can use Thrust for
extra power. They
are also strategic
fighters, using
Default at times.

Highly intelligent
as far as orcs go,
this leader uses
Encourage to raise
ally physical
attack and
Venomous Stab to
add a poison
effect to his
attacks.

Some sort of
strange power
animates this orc
corpse. While low
in intelligence,
it is still a
formidable foe
when it goes on a
rampage with
Weapon Whirl.

This ruler of the
orcs is the most
powerful of his
kind. He uses Mass
Attack to inflict
major damage and
Rampart to negate
physical attacks.

This swordsman of
the Eternian Sky
Knights uses Prey
on Weak to focus
on the foe with
the lowest
percentage of HP
left—not a very
knight-like
attack.

A spearman of the
Eternian Sky
Knights who uses
Mow Down to
eradicate multiple
foes in one sweep
of his long spear.

The archers of the
Eternian Sky
Knights have lower
strength and HP
than other Sky
Knights, but their
covering fire with
Poison Arrow must
not be taken
lightly.

This swordsman
hired by the
Merchantry uses
Final Strike, a
powerful attack
that comes last in
the action order.
Watch out for his
Carotid Slash—it
lowers magic
defense.

This brawler in
the hire of Khamer
& Profiteur
Merchantry uses a
variety of moves,
including Psych Up
to raise his own
physical attack
and Eye Gouge to
rob foes of their
sight.

This mage in the
hire of Khamer &
Profiteur is adept
in the use of
sleep to aid his
allies. You must
also beware of his
powerful fira
spell.

A spearwoman of
the Bloodrose
Legion who should
not be taken
lightly, lest you
end up on the
receiving end of
her Execution. Her
short haircut is
quite charming.

An archer of the
Bloodrose Legion
whose Chaos Arrow
attack uses
poison-tipped
arrows that sow
confusion. Don’t
let the sexy low
cut on her dress
back distract you!

A mage of the
Bloodrose Legion
who attacks with
gravity and aids
her allies with
silence. Her black
outfit is sure to
turn heads.

A swordsman of the
Black Blades armed
with a curved
blade shining with
an eldritch glow.
He uses Edge of
Madness to sow
confusion.

This spearman of
the Black Blades
must be attacked
with care, for a
physical attack on
him will be met
with a painful
Counter.

An axeman of the
Black Blades whose
fierce Shell Split
attack also lowers
physical defense.

A sword-wielding
guardsman of
Eternian Central
Command whose
Carotid Slash can
lower magic
defense. Beware of
situations where
his unit includes
a mage.

A pike-wielding
guardsman of
Eternian Central
Command whose
First Thrust lands
before anyone else
can act. It is
particularly
dangerous when
paired with Brave.

This magic-using
guardsman of
Eternian Central
Command aids his
allies with sleep
and attacks from
afar with
blizzaga.

This ordinary wild
wolf is not much
of a threat to
fully armored
warriors, but
those with little
or no armor should
fear its rather
powerful Bite
attack.

This wolf corpse
risen from the
dead as a rabid
beast uses the
ghastly
Self-Cannibalize
attack to raise
its physical
attack by
consuming part of
its own body.

This silver-coated
wolf may be a
sight to see, but
under its stunning
coat is the heart
of a killer. It
will mercilessly
devour its prey
after delivering a
Paralyzing Bite.

This vicious hound
employed by
Eternian Forces
attacks its
victims with Snap,
which makes them
more likely to be
targeted for
subsequent
attacks.

This hellhound is
a monstrous
defensive weapon
that attacks
entire parties
with <Hellfire>
and counters
physical attacks
with the powerful
Payback.

This unusually
long-whiskered
creature is hard
to hit with
physical attacks
because it raises
its evasion with
Footwork on top of
its already swift
movement.

A forest-dwelling,
puma-like monster
that can attack
two targets at
once with its
<Dual Attack>.

This tiger-like
monster inhabits
hot, arid regions.
The <Chomp> attack
it delivers with
razor-sharp fangs
has the potential
to kill its prey
in a single
strike.

The deadliest of
the panther-like
creatures, it has
a strange pattern
on its fur and
powerful attacks.
Watch out for its
<Blaster> attack,
which can paralyze
all its foes.

This squirrel-like
fairy uses cure.
Do not be
distracted by its
cute looks—defeat
it quickly, before
it heals its
allies.

This strange
little creature
uses the power of
a jewel within its
body to perform
magic. It supports
its allies with
powerful white
magic, but its
aeroga attack is
also dangerous.

This fairy of snow
and ice may look
cute, but it uses
powerful white
magic such as
curada, reflect,
and holy to
torment hapless
travelers.

This fairy takes
the form of a cat
and uses blind on
its foes, making
it the worst
nightmare of all
who rely on
physical attacks.
Have some eye
drops and blindna
on hand!

A cat fairy who
turned evil due to
human society’s
negative
influence. It
raises its allies’
strength with the
power of money,
and can hinder
magic with
silence.

A cait sith who
learned white
magic after much
grueling study. It
has mastered a
variety of spells,
including those
for recovery, aid,
and attack.

A cait sith who
studied under a
legendary feline
mage. This master
of black magic
uses fear to
strike dread into
its foes and death
to instantly
strike them dead.

This large,
savage,
razor-clawed bear
uses Salmon Swipe
to attack all its
foes. It can wipe
out your entire
party if you are
not careful.

This brutal weapon
in the guise of a
bear is stationed
all over Eternia
to deter invaders.
Its Bare Claws
attack ignores its
foes’ physical
defense and deals
major damage.

This savage, man-
eating bear looks
like it is wearing
a red helm and
uses Massacre, a
dangerous attack
in which it hits
random targets
four times with
its razor-sharp
claws.

This bull-headed
monster terrorizes
all who stumble
into its dungeon.
The destructive
power of its
Double Damage
hammer attack is a
serious threat.

A slain minotaur
that somebody
turned into a
zombie. Almost
nothing can stop
it once it raises
its physical
attack with
Berserk and goes
on a rampage.

This huge creature
with bull-like
horns and a large
beak uses Defang
to devastate its
opponent’s
physical attack
and Berserk to go
on a rampage.

This horned
monster, the most
powerful of its
bull-like
brethren, uses
Berserk Marathon
to raise its
physical attack
and Massacre to
hit random targets
four times.

A giant, razor-
fanged bat that
uses Blood Thirst
to damage foes and
recover its own
HP.

This bat craves
the blood of the
living and is
every mage’s bane
because it uses
Magic Thirst to
drain MP. Its
Venomous Fangs
attack is also a
threat.

This bat, raised
by Lord DeRosso as
one of his
minions, is the
most formidable of
its kind. It uses
Ultrasonic Wave to
confuse all its
foes.

This vicious
vulture circles
overhead,
searching for
victims on which
to use Carrion
Carnivore to feast
upon their flesh
and raise its own
physical attack.

This fearsome,
red-feathered
eagle must not be
taken lightly, for
its Dive attack
with its razor-
sharp beak is
fairly powerful.

It is said that
all who catch this
monstrous bird’s
sharp eyes can
only pray for a
swift death. Its
<Tornado> attack
strikes at all its
foes with whirling
blades of air.

Watch out for this
savage fish, for
it is known to
attack sailors
with its sharp
fangs and use
<Spray> to
penetrate even
Default defenses.

These monstrous
fish attack in a
large group that
surrounds and
sinks even larger
ships. Those hit
by Group Snap are
often targeted by
many more attacks.

This devil of the
sea brings fear
and death to
seafarers. Its
Gnash attack can
be devastating,
and it can also
use <Spray> to
penetrate even
Default defenses.

A giant, venomous
snake inhabiting
arid regions. Its
powerful attack,
Venomous Fangs, is
the bane of the
merchant caravans
that crisscross
Ancheim.

A giant sea snake
with sharp spikes
on its tail. Few
seafarers live to
tell of the
beast’s Tail
Shock, which
paralyzes the body
before the monster
goes in for the
kill.

This giant sea
snake has an
odious coloration.
Its Fatal Poison
causes the doom
state. Once
bitten, you should
try to end the
battle as quickly
as you can.

The most powerful
of the venomous
snakes, this
creature has
poison spines and
a deadly attack
called <Toxic
Whirl>, in which
it attacks and
poisons all its
foes.

This strange
frog-like creature
has sharp fangs,
but is rather
timid and tends to
run away quickly.
Its venom,
however, makes it
somewhat dangerous
when provoked.

This orange,
frog-like creature
has a powerful
attack known as
Rear-Ender. All
who come across
this creature with
its fully formed
backside soon wish
they hadn’t.

This frog-like
creature of sickly
coloration uses
Venomous Chomp to
cause various
status ailments.
It also counters
single attacks
with Don’t Touch.

A gigantic fish
found all over
Luxendarc’s seas.
Its Torpedo Drill,
in which it spins
its body like a
drill as it comes
stabbing with its
big horn, ignores
physical defense.

A member of the
monoceros family,
this vicious fish
uses Buzz Saw to
rip its foes to
shreds and rob
them of their
physical defense.

The most powerful
of the monoceros
family, this fish
has an attack
called Supersault,
which combines
forward/sideways
spins to create a
shock wave that
damages all foes.

This vicious shark
attacks sailors
with its countless
saw-like teeth and
uses Terror Fangs
to strike fear
into its victims.

Known as a living
fossil, this
species of shark
has survived since
ancient times. It
uses Ancient Fangs
to attack all its
foes and lower
their physical
attack and
physical defense.

The body of a dead
shark, reanimated
by some unknown
power. Its Fang
Rush strikes all
its foes with its
sharp fangs.

This huge turtle
attacks travelers
in mountain
passes. Its Spin
Attack unleashes a
blistering strike
that ignores
physical defense,
and Reflection
Angle creates a
reflection effect.

This huge turtle
evolved in mythril
mines, and while
its attack pattern
is the same as a
land turtle’s, its
physical attack
and physical
defense are
elevated by the
power of mythril
ore.

A huge, vicious
turtle with an
extremely hard
shell. It uses
Ground Spike for a
powerful attack on
all its foes and
Reflection Angle
to achieve a
reflection effect.

This gigantic,
razor-fanged sea
monster silences
its foes with its
mighty roar. Watch
out for its
venomous fangs,
and its Toothy
Rend is also a
major threat.

This monstrous
worm of the desert
is the bane of all
who use physical
attacks, for its
<Sandstorm> attack
blinds all it
strikes.

A giant worm that
eats and
fertilizes
Luxendarc’s soil.
Watch out for its
<Earthquake>
attack, which
strikes all its
foes. Your best
protection is some
way to reduce
earth attacks.

This man-eating
worm swallows its
prey whole and
uses <Gastric
Juices> to shoot
burning stomach
acid at all its
foes, damaging
them and lowering
their physical
defense.

The most powerful
of the giant
worms, it has an
odious coloration
and uses <Rock
Storm> to attack
all its foes with
rocks it has
sucked in.

A giant moth that
inhabits swamps
and other damp
places. Watch out
for its <Wing
Scales> attack,
which can confuse
its foes.

This giant,
poisonous moth is
the bane of all
who visit Florem.
Countless parties
have been wiped
out by its Poison
Scales for want of
some antidote.

This giant moth
with blazing wings
has a devastating
attack known as
<Firestorm>, which
damages all its
foes while also
lowering their
physical defense.

A giant scorpion
with a hard
exoskeleton and
large jaws. All
who face this
nightmare should
have measures in
place to deal with
its deadly
Venomous Bite.

A deadly scorpion
with huge,
hammer-like jaws
as hard as rock.
It uses Pheromone
to attract an ally
to help it attack
those who venture
into the forest.

This vicious
scorpion hunts its
prey in packs,
using Pheromone
Plus to attract
two allies. Try to
defeat it before
it can summon
help.

This king of the
scorpions has a
hard exoskeleton
and solid jaws. It
attracts allies
with Pheromone
Plus, then devours
them with
Cannibalism to
gain a big ability
boost.

A strange,
anthropomorphic
flower that walks
on roots. Its
<Paralyzing
Pollen> attack
renders victims
immobile. This is
quite dangerous if
they attack en
masse.

The dreaded
monstrous form of
the common
Eternian radish.
They attack en
masse, using
Poisonous Pollen
to damage and
poison their foes.

A deadly
carnivorous plant
that emits a sweet
scent from its red
flower. It uses
Parasitism to copy
the HP, MP, and BP
of its foe.

This monstrous
mushroom prefers
dank, dark places,
such as the forest
floor. Be careful
when striking it,
for its Spore
attack may cause
another myconid to
grow.

This ghastly,
monstrous mushroom
is purplish red.
When hit with a
physical attack,
it uses Chaos
Spore to release
confusion-causing
spores.

A monstrous
mushroom emitting
a faint glow in
the dark. When hit
with a physical
attack, it uses
High Voltage to
attack and
paralyze with
lightning spores.

Created when a
spirit possesses a
dead tree, this
monster uses
<Lullaby> to put
its foes to sleep
and then slowly
stabs them to
death with its
pointy branches.

This monstrous yet
bewitching tree
uses Sweet Scent
to charm people.
It also has a
creepy ability
known as Moan,
which lowers the
physical defense
of its foes.

A monstrous tree
that feeds on
those who get lost
in the forest. It
puts its foes to
sleep with
<Lullaby>, then
uses R.I.P. to
make sure they
never wake up.

This monster is a
corpse animated by
some dark power,
but its sluggish
movement keeps it
from being a great
threat. Its Rake
attack, however,
should not be
taken lightly.

These former
pirates, who
plundered across
the Sea of
Corsairs, are now
zombies with a
Double Damage axe
attack that is as
powerful as when
they still lived.

These fallen
soldiers are now
zombies that still
use Shield Bash,
an attack they
trained hard for
while alive. A
direct hit from it
paralyzes the
body.

Fallen soldiers
from a long-lost
land to the east
that have risen
again as zombies.
Their powerful
Rash Slash
sacrifices their
own defenses for
extra damage.

This disembodied
spirit of a dead
man uses <Curse>
to lower physical
attack and
physical defense.
It’s best to
defeat it quickly
before it can do
too much damage.

This disembodied
spirit of a dead
man who held a
terrible grudge
uses fear to
strike dread into
its foe and
<Curse> to lower
physical attack
and physical
defense.

An evil spirit
that tries to take
over a human body
by banishing its
soul. It uses
Death Clutch to
cause the doom
state and <Curse>
to lower physical
attack and
physical defense.

These animated
skeletons of
ancient knights
use Mass Attack to
attack all at once
and Flesh Flayer
to instantly halve
a foe’s HP.

An ancient
spearman
reanimated by a
dark power. Keep
an eye on your
remaining HP, for
its Bone Smash is
dangerous if your
HP is running low.

An ancient archer
reanimated by a
dark power. While
its attack does
not deal much
damage, watch out
for its Poisoned
Shot.

Though dead, this
skeleton of a
veteran knight has
not stopped
fighting. Its
Flesh Cleaver
takes half of its
target’s max HP.

The skeleton of a
spearman who
served a warlike
nation in ancient
times. It uses
<Bone Crush> to
deal damage equal
to max HP minus
current HP to all
foes.

The skeleton of an
archer who served
a powerful nation
that once
conquered half the
world long ago. It
uses Triple Shot
to strike a random
target with three
arrows.

This mischievous
little demon uses
black magic to
cause all sorts of
trouble. It is not
much of a threat
if it is prevented
from using magic.

This horse-faced
little demon
serves as a
familiar of the
Eternian Forces.
It uses thundaga,
powerful black
magic that can
damage all its
foes.

Said to be a
bringer of bad
dreams, this
little demon uses
powerful magic,
such as graviga
and aspir. It also
lowers its foe’s
physical attack
and physical
defense with
<Curse>.

Flying through the
air on black
wings, this
alluring little
demon uses Entice
to make victims do
as she says, which
is not always bad
if you like that
sort of thing.

Eternian Forces
use this little
demon against its
enemies. It uses
Tail Whip to charm
its foes, though
some may find its
backside just as
mesmerizing.

This little female
demon has a nice
tan and likes to
use aeroga. But of
far more interest
is her Nocturnal
Gala, an alluring
attack that charms
all her foes.

This mage of the
demon world sows
chaos with a
variety of magic,
including <Curse>
to lower physical
attack and
physical defense.
Also immobilizes
foes so that his
allies can attack.

A master of
advanced magic,
this dark mage not
only aids his
allies with blind
and veilga, he
uses comet to
directly join the
fray.

As cruel as he is
intelligent, this
archmage of
darkness uses
meteor, the most
powerful time
magic there is, to
plunge the enemy’s
entire party into
confusion.

This warrior demon
uses its brawny
body as a weapon
and terrorizes its
foes with Wing
Slice and Doom.

The Eternian
Forces use this
powerful demon for
melee combat. It
also uses fear to
fill enemies with
dread and Charming
Thrust to damage
and charm them.

The ultimate
demon, this fiend
pairs powerful
magic with epic
strength. He is a
master of thundaga
and can cause
instant death with
Cruel Thrust.

A small dragon
bred by the
Eternian Forces’
Monstrous Weapons
Division. Its
<Fireball> attack
penetrates its
foe’s defenses.

Further study has
spawned an evolved
d’gon with greater
firepower. Its
Fiery Breath can
strike all its
foes, and its
resistance to fire
has been improved.

This is the latest
d’gon that the
Eternian Forces
have spawned to
defend Central
Command. It slays
intruders with Icy
Breath, a water
attack that can
strike all its
foes.

Covered in a
tough, armor-like
hide, this sturdy
dragon has high
physical defense
in Default mode,
and its Rush
attack does twice
the damage as its
standard attack.

Named for its hard
plates, this
dragon has high
physical defense
in Default mode.
Its Double Rush
hits twice,
delivering double
the damage as its
standard attack
each time.

A dragon with
armor as sturdy as
a knight’s, and
high physical
defense in Default
mode. Its Rampage
does twice the
damage as its
standard attack
and strikes all
its foes.

A huge reptilian
creature with
sharp fangs and
great wings. It
has a Mow Down
attack that uses
its tail and an
attack called
Bully, which
attacks the lowest
HP enemy.

The Eternian
Forces created
this monstrous
weapon by feeding
a special chemical
agent to wild
dragons. Its Toxic
Breath damages and
poisons all its
foes, and it
sometimes comes
back to life even after being slain.

Said to spawn from
blazing flames,
this dragon is the
master of fire and
uses Scorching Eye
to boost all foes’
vulnerability to
fire, then
incinerates them
with Fiery Breath.

A dragon born of a
violent river,
filled with youth
and pride. It
boosts all foes’
vulnerability to
water with
Freezing Eye, then
immobilizes them
with Icy Breath.

A winged dragon
skilled at using
the forces of
wind. It can boost
all foes’ vulner-
ability to wind,
then use Stormy
Breath to create
an airless void to
annihilate them.

Spawned by the god
of earth, this
dragon uses Earth
Eye to boost all
foes’
vulnerability to
earth, then
paralyzes them
with an earth
shock wave from
Megaton Press.

Covered in shining
scales, this
celestial dragon
uses Celestial Eye
to boost all foes’
vulnerability to
light, then smites
them with the
blinding light of
Holy Breath.

This dark dragon
lives for the
carnage of battle.
It uses Evil Eye
to boost all foes’
vulnerability to
dark, then strikes
dread into them
with Fearsome
Breath.

A strange
gelatinous
creature that is
not much of a
threat itself, but
its Acid attack is
dangerous because
it lowers physical
defense.

This slime from
volcanic regions
poses a greater
threat due to the
use of <Venom> and
fira, particularly
in groups. Deal
with them swiftly
using magic or
other suitable
means.

A failed Eternian
experiment that
developed a will
of its own. It
uses the powerful
black magic dark
to attack, and
Blind to rob its
foes of their
sight.

Fire spirits have
gathered to form
this strange
sphere. It uses
Book Burning to
strike all its
foes with fire,
and it even has a
chance to prevent
magic use.

Water spirits have
gathered to form
this strange
sphere. Its
blizzara is a
powerful threat,
so use a lightning
attack to defeat
it quickly.

Wind spirits have
gathered to form
this strange
sphere. It uses
the aero magic
attack along with
a recovery ability
known as <White
Wind>.

Earth spirits have
gathered to form
this strange
sphere. It is
normally docile,
but attacks with
quaga when
approached. Boost
earth resistance
when coming near.

Lightning spirits
have gathered to
form this strange
sphere. It is
resistant to
physical attacks
and uses the magic
attack thunder.

Light spirits have
gathered to form
this strange
sphere. It uses
the white magic
attack holy and
can protect itself
by blinding
opponents with
Flash.

Dark spirits have
gathered to form
this strange
sphere. It uses
the black magic
fear to fill its
foes with dread.

Once a statue for
warding evil, this
creature was
transformed into a
living, breathing
monster by some
dark power. It can
support its allies
using protect and
sleep.

Some dark power
has driven this
ancient defense
system mad,
causing it to
terrorize its foes
with fear and
raise its allies’
evasion with the
time magic veilga.

Animated through
the prayers of the
shadowy ones, this
evil statue uses
doom, sleep, and
dark to
exterminate all
who oppose the
darkness.

This gigantic
humanoid weapon is
made of clay and
animated by magic.
Characters with
low physical
defense beware,
for its Megaton
Punch can prove
fatal, though it
is not very accurate.

Imbued with fire,
this new type of
golem has enhanced
combat abilities,
including Red Hot
to counter
physical attacks
and Flaming Blow
to hit individual
foes.

The Eternian
Forces have
deployed this new
golem to defend
Central Command.
It stops intruders
with Freezing
Punch and wipes
them out with
<Blizzard Blast>.

Developed by the
Eternian Forces,
this mechanical
soldier uses
Rocket Punch to
strike all its
foes, Limiter
Rescission to
raise physical
attack, and
Self-Repair to recover HP.

A new mechanical
soldier guarding
Eternia’s Central
Command. It
retains Rocket
Punch and Limiter
Rescission, and
adds Optical
Camouflage to
boost evasion.

This defender of
darkness is
modeled after
Eternia’s
mechanical
soldiers. It has
the features of
the Eternian
versions along
with a new one
called Overclocking, which accelerates its movement.

This brawny bearer
of the monk’s
asterisk uses
Invigorate to
raise his own
physical attack,
but when
desperate, he will
explode in a rage
that damages both
friends and foes.

This sexy,
sadistic bearer of
the white mage’s
asterisk uses a
variety of white
magic such as
cure, protect, and
aero. I wish I
could have met
her…

This petulant pyro
and bearer of the
black mage’s
asterisk is a
master of fire,
but has other
magic as
unpleasant as he
is, including
sleep, poison, and
silence.

This wily bearer
of the knight’s
asterisk and
master of sword
and shield knows
when to attack and
when to defend,
using just the
right ability each
time.

This cocky bearer
of the thief’s
asterisk will
steal your items
and use them
against you. His
high speed and
evasion make for a
bitter battle for
the heavily
armored.

Watch out for this
greedy bearer of
the merchant’s
asterisk. He uses
Takeover to deal
damage equal to
the money he
spends, ignoring
Default and
defenses.

This taciturn
bearer of the
spell fencer’s
asterisk uses
Sword Magic Fire
to enhance his
blade and
Sword Magic Silence
to prevent foes
from using magic.

This pompous
bearer of the time
mage’s asterisk
uses the time
magic Stop to
torment enemies.
He can also raise
his evasion with
veilga and attack
with quara.

This feral bearer
of the ranger’s
asterisk uses
Targeting to zero
in on her prey,
but when she gets
worked up, she
will unleash a
frenzied attack
called Multiburst.

This cruel,
twisted bearer of
the summoner’s
asterisk uses
Girtablulu, which
unleashes a
powerful earth
attack that hits
all foes, but has
a high cost
barring repeated use.

This noble bearer
of the valkyrie’s
asterisk is a
master of the
spear. She is
adept at
surprising foes
with Jump and
attacking all
enemies with
Crescent Moon.

This philandering
bearer of the red
mage’s asterisk
gets more BP when
suffering damage.
He boasts a wide
variety of moves,
such as
Captivating
Cologne, which
charms his foes.

This mad bearer of
the salve-maker’s
asterisk uses Dark
Breath, a
terrifying attack
born of his
compounding
skills. The less
HP he has, the
more damage it
will deal.

This bubbly bearer
of the performer’s
asterisk is a
teamwork pro. She
uses her singing
to dramatically
raise her allies’
physical attack
and increase BP,
then attack en
masse.

This hearty bearer
of the pirate’s
asterisk is a
master of reducing
enemy stats. He
uses Shell Split
to lower physical
defense and Defang
to lower physical
attack.

This bearer of the
ninja’s asterisk
toys with her foes
using Utsusemi,
which evades
physical attacks,
and Transience,
which lets her
counter after
evading.

This chivalrous
bearer of the
swordmaster’s
asterisk is a
master of
counterattacks.
His abilities
include Before
Swine, in which he
strikes back after
a magic attack,
and Know Thine Enemy, a powerful counter to an attack from the foe he selects.

This psychopathic
bearer of the
arcanist’s
asterisk has black
magic so powerful
it’s inhuman. She
uses poison
together with
Exterminate, which
damages poisoned
foes.

This scholarly
bearer of the
spiritmaster’s
asterisk is a
master of healing
and support. He
uses Fairy’s Aid
to enhance
elemental attacks
as well as
powerful white
magic including curada and holy.

This stoic bearer
of the templar’s
asterisk is a
seasoned champion
of attack and
defense. He uses
Default Guard to
heighten his
defenses in
Default mode and a
variety of attack
skills to do serious damage.

This bearer of the
dark knight’s
asterisk
sacrifices his own
HP in return for
the highest
physical damage of
the Eternian
Forces. His Minus
Strike, which
becomes more
powerful when he is in trouble, is also a threat.

This ancient
bearer of the
vampire’s asterisk
uses powerful
abilities from
monsters, such as
<Energy Burst> and
<Bone Crush>, to
make himself seem
more vampire-like.

This wise bearer
of the conjurer’s
asterisk is
powerful beyond
belief with his
Amped Strike or
meteor after he
raises his own
physical attack
with Invocation.

Airy’s hideous
larva-like form
uses Slaughter, an
attack with her
countless limbs
that has slain
countless warriors
of light.

Airy’s odious,
pupa-like form
uses powerful
attack magic along
with acedia, which
has a dispel
effect on all her
foes and leaves
them vulnerable to
all elements.

Airy’s final form
uses the ultimate
fire attack magic
Zeta Flare to
incinerate her
foes and the
deadly Plague
ability to
instantly slay
those with status
ailments.

This legendary
beast has one head
with the power of
<Hellfire> and
another with the
power of <Deep
Freeze>, but its
biggest threat is
Blazzard from both
heads.

Said to be born of
the gathered
malice of women
who have died by
drowning, this
enchantress uses
<Aqua Regia> to
lower the physical
defense of her
foes. Able to
transform at will,
she will sometimes hide in the ground or create mirror images of herself.

This ghastly
monster seeks to
suck up everything
in its path. Its
defenses are
rock-solid while
its eye is closed.
But when it opens,
watch out for its
<Energy Burst>,
which does major
damage to all its foes.

This undead mage
of the race of
giants uses
Negative Power
each turn to raise
his physical
attack and magic
attack before
unleashing his
mighty fists and
powerful magic.

The dark god of
destruction walks
among mortals in
human form and can
imitate the
abilities of the
foes he faces.

On the brink of
invading the
Celestial Realm,
the dark god of
destruction has
finally appeared
in his true form.
He uses Divergence
to separate his
foe from this
world, but it is
possible to weather the threat with aid from other worlds.

The manifestation
of gluttony, one
of the seven great
sins. Once a god
of fertile soils,
he was scorned and
labeled as one of
the most terrible
of all fiends,
ruler of demons
and the demon
world, when a foreign faith supplanted the native creed.

The manifestation
of pride, one of
the seven great
sins. A fallen
angel whose name
means “bringer of
light.” He was
once leader of all
angels, but was
exiled from the
heavens after
opposing his lord. He eventually became the leader of demons.

The manifestation
of wrath, one of
the seven great
sins. Once a great
angel who led
other angels in
service to the
lord creator, he
would later defy
his lord and
formed a
rebellious army of like-minded angels.

The manifestation
of lust, one of
the seven great
sins. Mentioned in
the records of the
Old Faith, he is
honored among the
demons of the
east, ranked
thirty-second
among all fiends,
and leads an army of seventy-two divisions.

The manifestation
of avarice, one of
the seven great
sins. The name
once simply meant
wealth or riches,
but over the
course of time the
idea was
personified as
Mammon, and
eventually she became one of the seven lords of hell.

The manifestation
of sloth, one of
the seven great
sins. Originally
he was an ancient
god, worshipped in
the times before
the Old Faith.
With the rise of
Crystal Adventism,
he came to be
regarded as a demon.

The manifestation
of envy, one of
the seven great
sins. Once the
name of a monster
that lived in the
ocean depths,
people began to
view him as a
demon and ally of
Satan, and thus he
secured his place as grand admiral of the demon navies.

A mad bird wearing
the white veil of
a bride. Its
insides are
decorated with
beads and shiny
objects. It has no
muscles or flesh,
and chains are
wrapped around its
feet. What does it
all mean?

A giant red fish,
with glittering
eyes and rows of
sharp teeth. Its
insides are filled
with toy
gemstones, and it
munches on
“comets” that swim
around it.
